mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations and raises borrowing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with UNRATE changes (Spearman rho > -0.15 over any 3-year window), or positive returns occur in > 60% of months following UNRATE increases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts measured by HOUST signal weakening residential demand and reduced construction activity, which cascades into lower employment and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following HOUST declines > 5% are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declining signals weakening manufacturing output and economic capacity utilization, which reduces corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods following INDPRO declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in payroll employment PAYEMS and rising unemployment UNRATE signals accelerating labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move adversely simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 0.2% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both PAYEMS declines > 0.2% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales RSAFS and housing starts HOUST signals broad-based demand collapse across consumer and residential sectors, which depresses corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%), elevated inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates further, signaling persistent price pressures that force the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by elevated GDPC1 growth, a sharp contraction in RSAFS signals demand destruction that reverses growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month in context of GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following sharp RSAFS declines during periods of positive GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
